# WNBA Card Collector — Session Progress Log
# Updated by Claude at the end of every session. Do not edit manually mid-session.
# Format: newest session at the top.

---

## Session: April 20, 2026 — Background Removal Dropped · No-Checklist Parallel ID

### Completed This Session

**Background removal removed entirely:**
- `@imgly/background-removal` uninstalled (23 packages removed) — was too aggressive, stripping card borders along with table surface; ML model trained on portraits/products, not flat cards
- `src/lib/imageProcessing.ts` rewritten to compression-only: `browser-image-compression` to 500KB / 1400px, returns `{ file, previewUrl }` (no `bgRemoved` field)
- `next.config.mjs` reverted to minimal — WASM webpack experiments block (`asyncWebAssembly: true, layers: true`) removed, no longer needed
- `src/app/scan/page.tsx` — removed all `bgRemoved` references: `ZoneProps`, `Zone` signature, overlay text (→ "Compressing…"), status badge logic, `frontBgRemoved`/`backBgRemoved` state, `handleFileSelected`/`clearFront`/`clearBack`/`reset` call sites

**scan-stage2 no-checklist parallel identification:**
- `supabase/functions/scan-stage2/index.ts` — added two-branch prompt logic:
  - `hasChecklist = true` (existing): structured DB parallel list → Claude matches to a `parallel_id`
  - `hasChecklist = false` (new): free-form visual description prompt; Claude uses Panini/Prizm industry naming conventions to guess `parallel_name`; `parallel_id` returns `null`
- Stage 2 now always runs an AI call regardless of whether parallels exist in DB

**Build:** `npm run build` passes clean.

**Stale cache fix:**
- Deleting `.next` after uninstalling `@imgly` resolved `Cannot find module './vendor-chunks/@swc.js'` 500 errors on `/checklist/[setId]`

### Decisions Made
- Compression only is the right pre-processing step for MVP; bg removal adds complexity and degrades card images when shot flat on a surface
- Stage 2 should always attempt parallel identification — returning a named guess (even with `parallel_id: null`) is more useful than a hard stop

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- `supabase functions deploy scan-stage2` failed: CLI not linked. Run `supabase link --project-ref <ref>` then redeploy both `scan-stage1` and `scan-stage2`

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Same as previous: all pending migrations (00001, 00002, 00003, 20260418000001) must be applied
- Edge Functions must be deployed after linking CLI

### Next Session: Start Here
1. **Checklist import screen — Beckett file variation handling:** small format differences between Beckett exports (column order, quoting, encoding, "Prizms" vs "Prizm" vs insert spacing) — review real-world CSVs and harden the parser
2. **Collection page refinements:** continue polish (filter wiring, grouping, visual improvements)
3. Apply all pending DB migrations
4. Deploy Edge Functions: `supabase link --project-ref <ref>` → `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`
5. Card Detail screen at `/collection/[id]` (Phase 3)

---

## Session: April 20, 2026 — Scan + Collection Polish · Image Processing Pipeline

### Completed This Session

**Packages installed:**
- `browser-image-compression` — client-side compress to ~400KB before AI pipeline
- `@imgly/background-removal` — WASM-based background removal (BRIA RMBG model, loads from CDN)
- `next.config.mjs` updated: `asyncWebAssembly: true, layers: true` for WASM support

**New file: `src/lib/imageProcessing.ts`**
- `processCardImage(file)` → compresses to 500KB, removes background, re-compresses PNG if >750KB
- Background removal is a lazy dynamic import (`await import('@imgly/background-removal')`) — never runs on server
- Failure-safe: any error in bg removal silently falls back to compressed original
- Returns `{ file, previewUrl, bgRemoved }` — caller revokes `previewUrl` when done

**Scan page (`src/app/scan/page.tsx`) — full visual rewrite matching `ScanScreen.jsx` design:**
- `ScanHeader` component: orange "SCAN CARD" supertitle + Barlow Condensed 34px uppercase title, changes per stage
- `Zone` component: dashed-border aspect-ratio capture zones (5/4 front, 7/3 back); camera icon circle (orange for required); Camera/Library ghost buttons inside zone
- `ProcessingSteps` component: step-progression list (Uploading → Identifying → Classifying) with teal checkmarks for completed steps, pulsing card preview with orange ring
- Success stage: radial teal+gold glow burst behind card, "★ Added to the Vault" badge, clean CTAs
- Confirming stage: expanding dot navigator (active dot widens to 20px), inline confidence badge, AI reasoning accordion, redesigned field grid
- Image processing pipeline:
  - On file select → immediately shows original preview, kicks off `processCardImage` async
  - `frontToken`/`backToken` refs prevent stale async results if user clears/re-selects
  - Zone shows "Removing background…" overlay with spinner + "First use may take a moment" note while processing
  - On complete → updates to processed preview + "✓ Background removed" / "✓ Compressed" badge
  - "Identify Card" button disabled while `frontProcessing === true`
  - `handleScan` uses `frontScanFile ?? frontFile` (processed file preferred; fallback to original)
  - Max raw file size guard: 25MB (prevents browser tab crash; compression handles rest)

**Collection page (`src/app/collection/page.tsx`) — polish:**
- Replaced Unicode view toggle icons (`⊞ ☰ ⫶`) with inline SVGs (`VIEW_ICONS` map)
  - Grid: 2×2 squares, List: 3 horizontal lines, Stack: 3 vertical bars of varying height
- Added "By Set" and "By Team" display chips (matching design — no active filter state for now)

**Build:** `npm run build` passes clean, all 16 routes. `/scan` is 36.4 kB (expected — new packages).

### Decisions Made
- Background removal happens **before** "Identify Card" tap (not during the AI pipeline) — user sees the processed card while waiting, and the scan button is not delayed by AI round-trips.
- Token-ref cancellation pattern: `++frontToken.current` on each new file selection; processing result ignored if token changed (user cleared or replaced image during processing).
- First CDN model download (~50MB) shown with "First use may take a moment" copy — no timeout enforced. Model is browser-cached for all subsequent scans.
- Transparent PNG output from bg removal is sent directly to Claude Vision — transparent areas don't interfere with card identification.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- "By Set" and "By Team" chips visible in Collection but not wired to filter logic (design shows them as inactive placeholders)

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Same as previous: all pending migrations + Edge Functions still need to be applied/deployed

### Next Session: Start Here
- Apply all pending DB migrations (00001, 00002, 00003, 20260418000001)
- Deploy Edge Functions: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`
- Implement Card Detail screen (`/collection/[id]` — Phase 3, `CardDetailScreen.jsx` in design bundle)
- Wire real Supabase data to Want List and Goals

---

## Session: April 19, 2026 — Phase 2.6 · Post-Design Code Review + Bug Fixes

### Completed This Session
- Ran `/review` against Phase 2.6 UI design implementation diff (39 files, ~10k insertions)
- Fixed all identified bugs in `src/app/collection/page.tsx`:
  - **`hits` filter semantic bug**: Replaced `c.cost > 200` cost proxy with correct card-collecting
    definition of "hit" — `isHit()` returns true when `serial !== null` (serially numbered card)
    OR `PARALLELS[parallel].run <= 99` (low-print-run numbered parallel). Extracted as module-level
    pure function for reuse across filter, chip count, and stat strip.
  - **`loadCollection` stale closure**: Inner async function called inside `useEffect` but missing
    from deps array. Wrapped in `useCallback` (stable: only closes over `supabase` singleton and
    stable `setState` functions), added to `useEffect` deps to satisfy exhaustive-deps.
  - **`fav: false` placeholder**: Added `// TODO: add favorites column to collection table` comment
    to mark it as intentional pending work.
  - **Big Hits chip count**: Added `count={cards.filter(isHit).length}` prop to match other chips.
  - **`hitsCount` stat strip**: Updated from filtered subset + cost check to `cards.filter(isHit).length`
    (always shows total hits, not filtered-down hits).
- Confirmed two review findings were false positives (no changes needed):
  - `isPremium` is NOT dead code — used at CardArt line 150 for holo-sweep overlay condition
  - Feature card destructuring is NOT unsafe — already guarded by `{feature && (` at line 491
- `npm run build` and `npx tsc --noEmit` both pass clean after fixes

### Phase Checklist Updates
- [x] Phase 2.6 post-implementation review completed
- [x] `hits` filter uses correct card-collecting semantics
- [x] `loadCollection` stale closure resolved with `useCallback`
- [x] Stat strip `hitsCount` corrected

### Decisions Made
- "Hit" definition: `serial !== null` OR `print_run <= 99`. No `auto`/`patch` field exists yet;
  print_run ≤ 99 is the correct proxy. Cost is irrelevant to card rarity classification.
- `isHit()` extracted at module level (not inside component) — pure function, no closure risk.
- `useCallback` deps are empty `[]` — `supabase` is a module-level singleton, `setCards`/`setLoading`
  are stable React dispatch functions. No re-execution triggered on re-renders.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- None

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Same as previous: all pending migrations + Edge Functions still need to be applied/deployed

### Lower-Priority Debt (Not Addressed This Session)
- `// MOCK` comments not added to want-list/goals pages
- `⊞ ☰ ⫶` Unicode view-toggle icons may render inconsistently cross-platform — inline SVGs preferred
- `(data as unknown as DBCollectionRow[])` cast — add TODO to regenerate `database.types.ts`

### Next Session: Start Here
- Apply all pending DB migrations (00001, 00002, 00003, 20260418000001)
- Deploy Edge Functions: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`
- Implement Card Detail screen (`CardDetailScreen.jsx` in `.design-bundle/` — 25KB, includes price sparkline, comp rows, sell sheet)
- Wire real Supabase data to Want List (`want_list` table exists; page currently uses mock `WANTS` array)
- Schema design + backend for Goals (no DB table yet; page uses mock `GOALS` data)

---

## Session: April 17, 2026 — Phase 2 · Code Review + Pre-Commit Fixes

### Completed This Session
- Ran `/review` against full Phase 2 diff — no PRs open, reviewed local uncommitted changes
- Fixed all HIGH/MEDIUM findings before committing:
  - **[HIGH] Image size guard**: Added 750KB limit in `handleFileSelected` before base64 conversion;
    user sees a clear error message. Rationale: Supabase Edge Function payload limit ~1MB;
    base64 overhead is ~33%, so 750KB raw → ~1MB encoded is the safe ceiling.
  - **[MEDIUM] Double-submit on confirm button**: Added `confirming` state; confirm button disabled
    with spinner while the `/api/scan/confirm` request is in flight. Matches the pattern
    `ManualEntryForm` already used for its submit button.
  - **[MEDIUM/LOW] Object URL leak**: `URL.revokeObjectURL` now called in `handleFileSelected`
    (when replacing a previously selected image) and in `reset()` (on scan-another / back).
- Replaced deprecated `SpinnerGap` with `CircleNotch` (Phosphor v2 rename); updated all 3 usages.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes clean after all changes.
- Committed all Phase 2 work as single commit `cd239de`.

### Phase Checklist Updates
- [x] Pre-commit review findings addressed
- [x] Image size enforcement (750KB, with user-facing error)
- [x] Double-submit guard on confirm
- [x] Object URL cleanup
- [x] Deprecated icon replaced

### Decisions Made
- 750KB image cap chosen over a resizing step to keep the client simple for MVP; resizing can be added in Phase 6 polish if users complain.
- Single commit for all Phase 2 work (rather than splitting review fixes): patch set is small and logically tied to the same feature.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- None

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Remaining MEDIUM (CORS wildcard on Edge Functions): acceptable for MVP, tighten when Vercel domain is known.
- Three migrations still need to be applied: 00001, 00002, 00003
- Edge Functions must be deployed: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`

### Next Session: Start Here
- Apply all three Phase 2 migrations against the Supabase project
- Deploy Edge Functions
- Smoke test: photograph a card → Stage 1 → Stage 2 → confirm → verify collection entry + needs_review flag
- Begin Phase 3: Want List & Goals

---

## Session: April 17, 2026 — Phase 2 · Bug Fixes + Auto-Create + Front/Back Support

### Completed This Session
- Added front + back image support throughout the full scan pipeline
  - Upload route accepts `front` + `back` multipart fields; stores both to Storage
  - Edge Functions accept `front_image_base64`, `back_image_base64` inline (bypasses Storage download)
  - Stage 2 prompt updated to use both images when back is provided
  - Scan page `ImageZone` component handles front/back capture separately
- Decoupled AI pipeline from Storage: images passed as inline base64 so scan works even if Storage upload fails
- Fixed "Failed to create scan session" (500): upload route was inserting `front_image_path`/`back_image_path` columns that don't exist until migration 00002 runs; removed them from insert
- Fixed "Edge Function returned a non-2xx status code": all Edge Functions now always return HTTP 200; errors returned in body as `{ error: "..." }` so callers always see the real message
- Fixed POST /api/scan/confirm 500: `upsertSet`/`upsertCard` were inserting `needs_review: true` before migration 00003 ran; fixed with two-step pattern (insert without column, then best-effort update that silently fails if column absent)
- Implemented auto-create checklist entries on confirm
  - `upsertSet()`: case-insensitive match on name + year; creates set if not found
  - `upsertCard()`: exact match on set_id + card_number; creates card if not found
  - Auto-created rows flagged with `needs_review = true` for later cleanup
  - Confirm always works — no checklist required
- Scan page shows note "will be added automatically and flagged for review" when card_id is null
- Added migration `20260417000002_phase2_back_image.sql`: `front_image_path` + `back_image_path` columns on `scan_sessions`
- Added migration `20260417000003_needs_review_flag.sql`: `needs_review boolean` on `sets` and `cards` + partial index
- Created `src/lib/database.types.ts`: full typed `Database` interface for all 13 tables, Row/Insert/Update types, convenience aliases

### Phase Checklist Updates
- [x] Front + back image capture in scan UI
- [x] Inline base64 image transport to Edge Functions (no Storage dependency)
- [x] Auto-create set/card on confirm (no checklist required)
- [x] needs_review flag for auto-created rows
- [x] Migration-resilient inserts (works at any migration state)
- [x] database.types.ts with full schema coverage

### Decisions Made
- All Edge Functions return HTTP 200 — errors in body. Supabase `functions.invoke` drops body on non-2xx, so this is required for error visibility.
- Auto-create on confirm is the right UX: never block a user from adding a card they physically own.
- `needs_review` flag (not a separate table) for cleanup queue — simple, queryable via partial index.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- None

### Blockers / Open Questions
- All three migrations (00001, 00002, 00003) must be applied in order
- Edge Functions must be deployed: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set as Supabase Edge Function secret

### Next Session: Start Here
- Smoke test full flow: upload front+back → Stage 1 → Stage 2 → confirm → collection entry
- Verify `needs_review` queue works (check sets + cards tables after scan)
- Begin Phase 3: Want List + Goals

---

## Session: April 17, 2026 — Phase 2 · Scan Pipeline

### Completed This Session
- Created Supabase Storage bucket migration (`20260417000001_phase2_storage.sql`)
  - `card-images` bucket, 10MB limit, JPEG/PNG/WebP/HEIC only
  - RLS: users can upload/read/delete their own images (path-scoped by user_id)
- Built `supabase/functions/scan-stage1/index.ts` Edge Function
  - Accepts session_id + image_url, downloads image from Storage
  - Calls claude-sonnet-4-6 Vision with Stage 1 system prompt
  - Tries to match result against checklist DB (cards table)
  - Saves scan_results rows + updates session status to `identified`
  - Returns top 3 candidates with confidence scores
- Built `supabase/functions/scan-stage2/index.ts` Edge Function
  - Accepts session_id + selected card identity
  - Fetches parallels from DB for the set; filters by print_run if serial detected
  - Calls claude-sonnet-4-6 Vision with Stage 2 system prompt + parallel descriptors
  - Updates rank-1 scan_result with parallel classification
  - Returns top 3 parallel candidates with reasoning
- Built `src/app/api/scan/upload/route.ts`
  - Accepts multipart form upload, validates file type
  - Uploads to `card-images/{user_id}/{timestamp}.{ext}` in Supabase Storage
  - Creates scan_session record, returns session_id + image_url
- Built `src/app/api/scan/confirm/route.ts`
  - Validates session ownership, writes to collection table
  - Marks scan_session confirmed + scan_result rank-1 confirmed=true
- Built full scan page UI (`src/app/scan/page.tsx`)
  - 5 stages: idle → uploading → identifying → classifying → confirming → success
  - Manual entry fallback ("None of these")
  - Editable fields on confirmation: player, set, card#, serial, condition, cost, date
  - Confidence indicator (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) with color coding
  - Expandable AI reasoning panel
  - Candidate navigator (prev/next for top 3 candidates)
  - Low-confidence warning banner
- Updated `tailwind.config.ts` with full WNBA color palette + font-display variable
- Updated `src/styles/globals.css` with WNBA dark theme, `.input-field` component class
- Updated `src/app/layout.tsx` with Barlow Condensed + Inter fonts (Google Fonts)
- Excluded `supabase/functions` from Next.js tsconfig (Deno code, not Node)
- TypeScript: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes clean

### Phase Checklist Updates
- [x] Phase 1 complete
- [x] Supabase Storage bucket migration for card images
- [x] Image upload API route (camera/photo picker)
- [x] Stage 1 Edge Function (Claude Vision — card identity)
- [x] Stage 2 Edge Function (Claude Vision — parallel classification)
- [x] Serial number detection + candidate filtering logic
- [x] Scan confirmation UI (top 3 candidates, confidence, reasoning)
- [x] Confirm → write to collection
- [x] "None of these" → manual entry fallback
- [x] Scan session logging

### Decisions Made
- Used `claude-sonnet-4-6` (not `claude-3-5-sonnet` as originally specified in PRD — updated to current model)
- Edge Functions use Deno URL imports; excluded from Next.js tsconfig
- Storage paths scoped as `{user_id}/{timestamp}.{ext}` for RLS enforcement
- Stage 2 skips if no matched card_id from Stage 1 (avoids noise on unknown cards)
- Manual entry requires card to exist in checklist (keeps collection data integrity)

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- None — Phase 2 fully implemented

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Supabase Storage bucket must be created by running the migration (`20260417000001_phase2_storage.sql`)
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set in Supabase Edge Function environment secrets
- Edge Functions must be deployed: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`

### Next Session: Start Here
- Deploy Edge Functions to Supabase
- Run Phase 2 storage migration
- Smoke test full scan flow: upload → stage1 → stage2 → confirm → collection
- Begin Phase 3: Want List + Goals

---

## Session: April 18, 2026 — Phase 2.5 · Checklist Management

### Completed This Session
- Built full checklist management feature (Phase 2.5)
- Created `/checklist` page with two tabs: Sets list + Import CSV
  - Sets list: grouped by year, shows card/parallel counts per set, links to detail
  - Import tab: file picker, form fields (set name, year, manufacturer), result summary with error list
- Created `/checklist/[setId]` page with two tabs: Parallels + Cards
  - Parallels tab: color-coded badges using parallel color system from styling-guidelines.md, print run display
  - Cards tab: searchable/paginated table (50 per page), player name + card number + team + RC indicator
  - Both tabs have "Add" modals for manual entry
- Built API routes:
  - `GET/POST /api/checklist/sets` — list all sets with counts, create set
  - `GET/PATCH /api/checklist/sets/[setId]` — set detail with parallels + cards
  - `POST/PATCH /api/checklist/sets/[setId]/parallels` — create/update parallel
  - `GET/POST /api/checklist/sets/[setId]/cards` — paginated cards, create card
  - `POST /api/checklist/import` — bulk CSV import (Beckett format)
- Built CSV import pipeline:
  - Parses Beckett-format CSV (Card Set, Card Number, Athlete, Team, Sequence)
  - Greedy shortest-match root insert detection — correctly handles "Clark-Mania! Gold Vinyl"
    as insert=Clark-Mania!, parallel=Gold Vinyl (not a separate insert)
  - Creates one `sets` row per insert (Base → "2024 Panini Prizm WNBA",
    Fearless → "2024 Panini Prizm WNBA — Fearless", etc.)
  - Extracts all unique parallels per insert with print_run + is_numbered from Sequence field
  - Deduplicates cards by card_number within each insert-set
  - Inserts in 200-row batches; returns counts + any per-batch errors
  - Handles repeated imports (skip-if-exists for sets, parallels, cards)
- Added migration `20260418000001_parallels_write_policy.sql`: write RLS policy for parallels
  (init migration only added SELECT; import pipeline needs INSERT)
- Added "Manage Checklists" link to Collection page header
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes clean

### CSV Parsing Details
- 6,702 rows in 2024 Prizm CSV → 12 inserts, 12–33 parallels each
- Insert detection: greedy shortest-match algorithm on non-Prizms Card Set values
- Sequence parsing: "/99" → print_run=99, "1/1" → print_run=1

### Phase Checklist Updates
- [x] Admin/Checklist page layout with Sets + Import tabs
- [x] Set management UI (create, list, detail)
- [x] Parallel management UI (add parallel per set)
- [x] Card management UI (add card, paginated search)
- [x] CSV import (Beckett format — primary format)
- [x] RLS policy for parallels write

### Decisions Made
- One `sets` DB row per insert (not per product) — keeps unique(set_id, card_number) clean
  and matches how Stage 1 AI would identify cards by insert name.
- Base insert uses the provided set name directly; others get " — InsertName" suffix.
- Greedy shortest-match root detection prevents false positives like "Clark-Mania! Gold" as root.
- No XLSX support in this phase — CSV import sufficient for Beckett data exported to CSV.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- Parallel descriptors (color, finish, notes) populated structurally but empty — need manual fill
  or a follow-up seed pass from PRD Appendix A data
- Bulk load of 2024 Prizm CSV not yet executed against Supabase (migration 00001 still pending)

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Three migrations still need to be applied: 00001, 00002, 00003, and new 20260418000001
- Edge Functions still need to be deployed

### Next Session: Start Here
- Apply all four migrations against the Supabase project
- Deploy Edge Functions: `supabase functions deploy scan-stage1 scan-stage2`
- Import 2024 Prizm CSV via /checklist → Import tab
- Verify Stage 2 classification works with populated parallels
- Begin Phase 3: Want List & Goals

---

## Session: April 19, 2026 — UI/UX Design Implementation

### Completed This Session
- Fetched and extracted the claude.ai/design bundle (tar.gz) — 6 screen prototypes + chat transcripts
- Read all design files: CardArt.jsx, CollectionScreen.jsx, ScanScreen.jsx, CardDetailScreen.jsx,
  WantListScreen.jsx, GoalsScreen.jsx, EbayListingsSheet.jsx, Chrome.jsx, data.js, styles.css
- Implemented hi-fi WNBA UI across the entire app:

**New files:**
- `src/lib/cardData.ts` — TEAMS (15 franchises) + PARALLELS (11 types) constants;
  `nameToParallelKey()` maps Supabase parallel name strings → CardArt keys
- `src/components/CardArt.tsx` — Visual card component: team-color gradient faces,
  holographic sweep overlay, rainbow/mojo/shimmer parallel treatments,
  photographed-card presentation (cast shadow + top-edge reflection)
- `src/components/ParallelBadge.tsx` — Animated parallel pill badges (mojo-gradient,
  rainbow animated, shimmer sweep, color-coded per parallel)

**Updated files:**
- `src/styles/globals.css` — Added card animation keyframes: rainbow-border, holo-sweep,
  mojo-shift, shimmer-sweep, tilt-card hover (perspective rotateX/Y), alertPulse
- `src/components/BottomNav.tsx` — Redesigned to 4-tab layout (Collection, Want List,
  Scan FAB, Goals); orange Scan button elevated with glow shadow, matching design exactly.
  Removed eBay from nav (deprioritized for MVP)
- `src/app/collection/page.tsx` — Full redesign: "The Vault" header with BR avatar,
  stat strip (cards / value / hits), search bar, filter chips (All/Rookies/Premium/Big Hits),
  grid/list/stack view toggle; all wired to real Supabase data via updated query
- `src/app/want-list/page.tsx` — Full WantList redesign: animated alert strip,
  price-progress bars, priority dots, in-range highlighting; mock data (backend TBD)
- `src/app/goals/page.tsx` — Full Goals redesign: rainbow chase featured tile
  with color swatch slots, SVG progress rings; mock data (backend TBD)
- `src/app/page.tsx` — Redirects to /collection

**Build:** `npm run build` passes clean; all 16 routes compile.

### Design → Code Mapping
- Collection page maps Supabase DB rows to CardData shape:
  `parallel_id → parallels.name → nameToParallelKey()` → visual treatment
- DB fields used: serial_number, condition, cost_paid, acquisition_date,
  cards(player_name, team, card_number, rookie_card, sets(name, year)), parallels(name, print_run)

### Decisions Made
- CardArt uses inline CSS (not Tailwind) to match design pixel-for-pixel — the animations
  and gradients require dynamic values that Tailwind can't express cleanly at runtime.
- eBay tab removed from BottomNav to match design (Phase 4 feature, not MVP nav item).
- Want List and Goals use mock data from design prototype — real Supabase backend is Phase 3/4 work.
- Home page (`/`) redirects to `/collection` per the design's mental model.

### In-Flight / Partially Done
- Card Detail screen not yet implemented (design file: CardDetailScreen.jsx)
- Scan page visual enhancement deferred (logic works; styling is functional but not design-polished)
- eBay page not yet redesigned

### Blockers / Open Questions
- Same as previous session: migrations + Edge Functions still need to be applied/deployed

### Next Session: Start Here
- Apply all pending migrations and deploy Edge Functions (prerequisite for real scan flow)
- Implement Card Detail screen (CardDetailScreen.jsx in design bundle)
- Wire real Supabase data to Want List (want_list table exists in schema)
- Wire real Supabase data to Goals (no table yet — needs schema design)
- Visual polish on Scan page to match ScanScreen.jsx design

---
<!-- PASTE ADDITIONAL SESSIONS ABOVE THIS LINE -->
---
